user_rights: You have the right to return defective goods and claim a full refund within 30 days.
steps: Contact seller support with order ID and proof
Escalate to platform grievance officer within 48 hours
File complaint on National Consumer Helpline (1800-11-4000)
Approach District Consumer Commission if unresolved in 30 days

applicable_law: RBI Circular on Customer Protection, 2017 & Payment and Settlement Systems Act 2007
user_rights: Zero liability if reported within 3 working days. Limited liability if reported within 4-7 days.
steps: Block the card/account immediately through bank app or helpline
Report to the bank within 3 days for zero liability
File FIR with local police if amount exceeds ₹25,000
Approach Banking Ombudsman if bank doesn't resolve in 30 days
File complaint on cyber crime portal (cybercrime.gov.in)

applicable_law: Information Technology Act 2000, Section 66D & Indian Penal Code 420
user_rights: Right to file complaint for cheating and impersonation under IT Act and IPC.
steps: Take screenshots of the transaction and chat conversations
Report to UPI app customer care immediately
File complaint on National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal
Contact your bank to block the account if compromised
File FIR with local police station

applicable_law: DGCA Civil Aviation Requirements 2019 & Carriage by Air Act 1972
user_rights: Full refund for flight cancellations by airline. Compensation for delays over 2 hours.
steps: Check airline's refund policy and cancellation rules
File refund request through airline website/app
If denied, approach Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA)
File complaint on AirSewa portal (airsewa.gov.in)
Approach Consumer Commission for unresolved issues

applicable_law: Consumer Protection Act 2019 & Hotel (Regulation of Accommodation) Act
user_rights: Right to receive services as promised and get refund for cancellations as per policy.
steps: Document the booking confirmation and payment proof
Contact hotel customer service immediately
If using booking platform, escalate to their grievance officer
File complaint on Ministry of Tourism portal
Approach Consumer Commission if unresolved

user_rights: Service charge is optional, not mandatory. GST must be charged correctly on actual amount.
steps: Check bill for correct GST calculation (5% for restaurant, 18% for alcohol)
Refuse to pay mandatory service charge (it's voluntary)
Take photo of the bill and menu prices
Complain to restaurant manager
File complaint on National Consumer Helpline or GST grievance portal

applicable_law: Food Safety and Standards Act 2006 & IPC Section 272/273
user_rights: Right to safe and hygienic food. Can claim compensation for food poisoning.
steps: Seek immediate medical attention if food poisoning suspected
Preserve food sample if possible
Take photos of the food and restaurant conditions
File complaint with Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI)
File FIR if serious health issues occur
Approach Consumer Commission for compensation

applicable_law: Clinical Establishments Act 2010 & Consumer Protection Act 2019
user_rights: Right to standardized rates and transparent billing. Can challenge unnecessary procedures.
steps: Request detailed bill with break-up of all charges
Verify rates with government schedule if applicable
Take second opinion for expensive procedures suggested
File complaint with hospital's grievance redressal cell
Approach State Medical Council for malpractice
File complaint on National Consumer Helpline

applicable_law: Clinical Establishments Act 2010 & Insurance Regulatory Act 1999
user_rights: Right to emergency medical care without prepayment. Insurance companies must follow IRDAI guidelines.
steps: Document emergency nature of treatment required
If insurance denied cashless, pay and claim reimbursement later
File complaint with insurance company's grievance officer
Approach IRDAI grievance redressal cell
File complaint on Consumer Commission for denied emergency treatment

applicable_law: Motor Vehicles Act 1988 & Central Motor Vehicles Rules 1989
user_rights: Right to contest incorrect traffic challans within specified time period.
steps: Verify the challan details on official traffic police website
Check if vehicle was actually at location during violation time
File objection through official e-challan portal
Provide evidence of incorrect challan (photos, location proof)
Approach traffic court if objection is rejected

user_rights: Right to claim compensation for accident damages and injuries through insurance.
steps: File FIR immediately after accident
Inform insurance company within 24 hours
Collect evidence: photos, witness details, police report
Submit claim with all documents to insurance company
If claim rejected, approach Insurance Ombudsman
File case in Motor Accident Claims Tribunal for compensation

applicable_law: Payment of Wages Act 1936 & Industrial Disputes Act 1947
user_rights: Right to timely payment of wages and protection from wrongful termination.
steps: Document all salary delays and unpaid amounts
Send formal written complaint to HR/management
File complaint with Labor Commissioner of your state
Approach Labor Court for unresolved disputes
File case under Industrial Disputes Act if terminated unfairly

applicable_law: Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace Act 2013 & Equal Remuneration Act 1976
user_rights: Right to safe workplace environment and protection from harassment/discrimination.
steps: Document incidents with dates, times, witnesses
File complaint with Internal Complaints Committee (ICC)
If no action, approach Local Complaints Committee
File police complaint for criminal offenses
Approach Women's Commission for gender-based issues

user_rights: Right to timely possession as per agreement and interest for delays.
steps: Check project registration on RERA website
Document delay with possession agreement dates
File complaint on state RERA website
Claim interest for delay as per RERA rules
Approach RERA Appellate Tribunal if unsatisfied

applicable_law: Rent Control Acts (State-specific) & Transfer of Property Act 1882
user_rights: Protection against illegal eviction and right to timely refund of security deposit.
steps: Review rent agreement terms carefully
Send legal notice for security deposit refund
File complaint in Rent Control Court/Authority
Approach Civil Court for eviction disputes
File case in Consumer Commission for unfair practices

applicable_law: Prohibition of Unfair Practices in Technical Educational Institutions 2018
user_rights: Right to admission without capitation fees and transparent admission process.
steps: Document all fee payments and communications
File complaint with University Grants Commission (UGC)
Approach AICTE for technical institutions
File complaint on Consumer Helpline for refund issues
Approach Education Department of state government

applicable_law: Right to Information Act 2005 & University Regulations
user_rights: Right to fair evaluation and access to evaluated answer sheets.
steps: Apply for revaluation within university deadline
File RTI application to access answer sheets
Approach University Grievance Redressal Cell
File complaint with University Grants Commission
Approach High Court for serious evaluation issues

applicable_law: Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) Act 1997
user_rights: Right to accurate billing and quality service as per TRAI regulations.
steps: File complaint with telecom operator customer care
Escalate to appellate authority within operator
File complaint on TRAI grievance portal
Approach Consumer Commission for unresolved issues
Use Telecom Consumer Grievance Redressal Mechanism

user_rights: Right to privacy and protection from unsolicited communications.
steps: Report spam calls/messages to TRAI DND registry
File complaint on National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal
Approach Telecom Regulatory Authority for privacy violations
File police complaint for serious privacy breaches
Use Data Protection Bill provisions when enacted
